question_data.stem: Listen/read: “The policy is less a rupture than a recalibration of existing priorities.” What does the speaker mean? (C2 skill check 1)
options: It adjusts rather than completely changes policy.
It is unrelated to previous priorities.
question_data.originalContentNotice: Original item created for British English Academy ESL platform seed bank.

question_data.stem: Choose the most precise response in a high-level debate. (C2 skill check 2)
options: That distinction is useful, but it risks overstating causality where the evidence shows correlation.
You are kind of wrong maybe.
It is just not good evidence thing.
I disagree because it sounds strange.
question_data.originalContentNotice: Original item created for British English Academy ESL platform seed bank.

question_data.stem: Choose the most sophisticated written formulation. (C2 skill check 3)
options: The evidence warrants cautious optimism rather than an unequivocal claim of success.
The evidence says success totally but maybe not.
It is good evidence for good results.
Success is very clear in a cautious way.
question_data.rationale: Write with precision and rhetorical control.
question_data.originalContentNotice: Original item created for British English Academy ESL platform seed bank.
